body:
## Summary

Fixes #687. When Formal AI is driven as an **agentic backend** (e.g. OpenCode over the OpenAI-compatible server), four ordinary prompt classes dead-ended on the deterministic "I could not determine…" blurb — including, ironically, *asking it to report the bug on GitHub*.

**Root cause:** `plan_chat_step` (the agentic planner) had no recipe for these request classes, so it returned `None`, the loop fell through to the symbolic solver, and the solver — unable to answer a factual, report, research, or recall prompt from local Links Notation rules — emitted the unknown blurb. OpenCode faithfully rendered that blurb; the harness was not at fault (mirrors the #676 conclusion).

## What changed

Three new deterministic planner recipes, each emitting the tool calls the harness executes (Formal AI has **no HTTP client of its own** by design):

| Request class | Module | Behaviour |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Factual / research question | `src/agentic_coding/web_research.rs` | `websearch` → `webfetch` the surfaced source → answer from it |
| "Report [this] on GitHub" | `src/agentic_coding/report_issue.rs` | `gh issue create --repo link-assistant/formal-ai …`, then surface the created URL |
| "What were we talking about?" | `src/agentic_coding/conversation_recall.rs` | Final answer from message history; no tool call |

**Generalization, not a phrase list (issue requirement R1):** web research fires only when the symbolic engine reports it cannot resolve the prompt locally (`FormalAiEngine.answer(task).intent ∈ {unknown, web_search}`), reusing the existing seed-backed `detect_web_search_query`. "What is the capital of France?" (intent `fact_lookup`) still resolves locally and is **not** sent to the web.

**All environments (R6/R10):** the browser worker is WASM-compiled from the same Rust planner (`mode = "wasm worker"`), so these recipes reach the web/desktop environment automatically; the web UI shell (`src/web/app/main.jsx`) already recognised report-issue and recall independently.

## Reproduction & tests

`tests/unit/issue_687.rs` drives `plan_chat_step` exactly as an agentic CLI would — one test per reported prompt class (7 tests). Every test fails on `main` (planner returns `None`) and passes with the fix.

```
cargo test --test unit issue_687   # 7 passed
```

## Case study

Deep case study compiled to `docs/case-studies/issue-687/` per the issue requirement: `README.md`, `requirements.md` (all 11 requirements itemised), `root-cause.md`, `online-research.md` (US election facts corroborated: next general election **Nov 3, 2026**; existing-components survey), `upstream.md`, and `raw-data/` (issue/PR/CI JSON + the session screenshot).

## Verification

- `cargo test` — full suite passes (one HTTP integration test is flaky under parallel socket load; passes in isolation, unrelated to this change)
- `cargo fmt --check` — clean
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` — clean
- `scripts/check-file-size.rs` — all files within limits
- No new Cargo dependencies (only `serde_json`, already vendored)
title: Agentic mode: act on simple requests (report issue / web research / recall) (#687)
